title: Red Rock Road Trip
subtitle: 3 days in Zion and Bryce Canyon

team.note: Sam and Theo are chasing the big climbs. Maya and Ana are here for the easy trails and the views. Everyone meets up for the Narrows and the sunrises.

bring: Broken-in hiking shoes and blister tape
3 liters of water per person (desert heat)
Quick-dry layers and a rain shell for the Narrows
Headlamp for the pre-dawn Bryce sunrise
Reusable bottles and a couple of trail snacks

bookings.title: Cable Mountain Lodge, 2 nights
bookings.note: Steps from the Zion pedestrian entrance.

bookings.title: Oscar's Cafe dinner
bookings.note: Ask for the patio if the wind is calm.

bookings.title: Angels Landing permit
bookings.note: Seasonal lottery on recreation.gov. Apply the day before by 4pm Mountain.

bookings.title: Bryce Canyon Lodge room
bookings.note: Books out months ahead. We are on the cancellation list, otherwise it is Ruby's Inn.

lists.title: The whole point
lists.note: The reasons we drove all this way. Do not leave without them.

items.text: Summit Angels Landing and clip the chains

items: Wade up the Narrows in the Virgin River
Catch first light over the hoodoos at Bryce
Walk the Navajo Loop down into the amphitheater

lists.title: Bonus adventures
lists.note: Would be amazing if the light and the legs cooperate.
items: Stargaze at Bryce (it is a Dark Sky park)
Drive the Zion-Mount Carmel tunnel at golden hour
Cowboy breakfast at Ruby's before the drive home

notes: Zion Canyon is car-free April to October. Ride the free shuttle from the visitor center.
Flash floods can close the Narrows with no warning. Check the ranger forecast that morning.
The Springdale to Bryce leg is a real drive. The Schedule tab flags any day that is too tight.
closingNote: Dusty boots, full memory cards, safe drive home.

places.name: Cable Mountain Lodge
places.desc: Home base for the two Zion nights.

places.name: Zion Visitor Center
places.desc: Free shuttle hub. No private cars up the canyon in summer.

places.name: The Grotto (Angels Landing)
places.desc: Trailhead for Angels Landing. Permit required for the chains.

places.name: Emerald Pools
places.desc: Shaded, easy loop to waterfalls and pools.

places.name: Temple of Sinawava
places.desc: Last shuttle stop and the gateway to the Narrows.

places.name: Oscar's Cafe
places.desc: Green chile burgers and big portions in Springdale.

places.name: Bryce Sunrise Point
places.desc: The classic first-light view over the amphitheater.

places.name: Navajo Loop, Sunset Point
places.desc: Switchbacks down past Thor's Hammer into the hoodoos.

places.name: Ruby's Inn, Bryce
places.desc: Diner, general store, and the backup beds.

places.name: Bryce Visitor Center
places.desc: Maps, water, and the shuttle for the amphitheater.

days.title: Arrive Zion
notes: Check-in is 3pm. Park once and take the shuttle from here on.

schedule.title: Shuttle up the canyon and Emerald Pools
schedule.note: Easy first hike to shake out the drive.

days.title: Big Zion day
notes: The group splits in the morning and meets back up for the Narrows.

schedule.title: Angels Landing and the chains
schedule.note: Permit hike. Start early, it bakes and backs up by late morning.

schedule.title: Lower and Middle Emerald Pools
schedule.note: The mellow parallel plan while the others climb.

schedule.title: Wade the Narrows
schedule.note: Check the flash flood forecast before heading up.

days.title: Zion to Bryce
days.closingNote: Safe drive home. Cowboy breakfast optional but encouraged.
notes: This is the ambitious day. Let the timing check tell you what actually fits.

schedule.title: First light at Bryce Sunrise Point
Drive home
schedule.note: Fill the tank in Panguitch. Long haul from here.
